raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Pedestrian fatalities reached a four-decade high in 2020, with California having the most pedestrian fatalities in the nation.",
    "Vehicles have been steadily increasing in size and weight, with some models now weighing several thousand pounds.",
    "The California Transportation Commission will study the relationship between vehicle size and injuries to vulnerable road users.",
    "Vehicle sizes have increased dramatically since the 1980s.",
    "The study aims to develop policies that will help keep drivers and vulnerable road users safe on roads.",
    "On average, a pedestrian was killed every 81 minutes and injured every 10 minutes in traffic crashes in 2020.",
    "Pedestrian deaths accounted for 17% of all traffic fatalities and 2% of all people injured in traffic crashes.",
    "Late-model SUVs may be more likely to kill pedestrians than cars.",
    "AB 251 would study the costs and benefits of imposing a vehicle weight fee.",
    "At least 13 states charge weight-based registration fees for vehicles."
  ]
}
```
